portfolio_rationale: AMZN earns the top spot with the highest judge confidence (0.85) among bullish signals, supported by both strong fundamentals and improving technical momentum (price above 20-day MA, MACD above signal). XOM ranks second with a clean bullish technical picture (price above all key MAs, positive 20-day momentum) and positive cash flows, though fundamentals are thinner. GOOGL and LLY are both bullish but carry heavier technical headwinds and lower conviction; LLY's bear case is particularly strong given the technical analyst's 0.9 confidence bearish signal. AAPL is neutral per the judge and receives no weight; BRK.B is explicitly bearish and receives no weight.

AMZN.reason: Highest judge confidence (0.85) among bullish names, strong fundamentals with 15.7% revenue growth, improving technicals with price above 20-day MA and MACD above signal line.

XOM.reason: Bullish judge signal (0.70 confidence) with the cleanest technical picture in the universe — price above 20/50-day MAs, EMA12 above EMA26, and positive 20-day return momentum.

GOOGL.reason: Bullish judge signal (0.70 confidence) backed by strong ROE/ROA and solid liquidity, though all technical indicators are bearish and warrant a reduced position.

LLY.reason: Bullish judge signal but lowest conviction (0.60) with a technically bearish analyst at 0.9 confidence and all momentum indicators negative; small position reflects the weak risk/reward balance.

AAPL.reason: Judge signal is neutral (0.50 confidence); bull and bear cases are balanced with negative price momentum and liquidity risk unresolved.

BRK.B.reason: Judge signal is explicitly bearish (0.65 confidence) with negative revenue growth, thin margins, majority-negative news sentiment, and all momentum indicators pointing down.
